records.ety: [OE. serchen, cerchen, OF. cerchier, F. chercher, L. circare to go about, fr. L. circum, circa, around. See Circle.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Searched (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Searching.]

defs: To look over or through, for the purpose of finding something; to examine; to explore; as, to search the city.
quotes: They are come to search the house.
Search me, O God, and know my heart.

defs: To inquire after; to look for; to seek.
quotes: I will both search my sheep, and seek them out.
Enough is left besides to search and know.

defs: To examine or explore by feeling with an instrument; to probe; as, to search a wound.
To examine; to try; to put to the test.
To seek; to look for something; to make inquiry, exploration, or examination; to hunt.
quotes: It sufficeth that they have once with care sifted the matter, and searched into all the particulars.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. cerche. See Search, v. t.]

defs: The act of seeking or looking for something; quest; inquiry; pursuit for finding something; examination.
quotes: Thus the orb he roamed With narrow search, and with inspection deep Considered every creature.
Nor did my search of liberty begin Till my black hairs were changed upon my chin.
